Debug logging macros and functions for initialization and printing the last launched app.

# Purpose
This C header file defines a simple interface for debugging functions related to logging and application launch tracking. It includes a conditional macro `DEBUG_LOG` that facilitates logging messages with a specified log level, file name, and line number if `PBL_LOG_ENABLED` is defined. The file also declares two functions: [`debug_init`](<#debug_init>), which initializes debugging with a given `McuRebootReason`, and [`debug_print_last_launched_app`](<#debug_print_last_launched_app>), which outputs information about the last launched application. The file includes necessary headers for microcontroller reboot reasons and standard integer types.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `drivers/mcu_reboot_reason.h`
- `stdint.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### debug\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:debug_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/debug/debug.h#L31>)

Initializes the debug logging system with reboot information.
- **Description**: Use this function to set up the debug logging system at the start of a program. It logs the firmware version, hardware platform, and build ID, and records the reason for the most recent microcontroller reboot. This function should be called early in the system initialization process to ensure that all relevant startup information is captured in the logs.
- **Inputs**:
    - `reason`: Specifies the reason for the microcontroller's last reboot. This parameter must be a valid `McuRebootReason` value. The function logs this information for diagnostic purposes.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`debug_init`](<debug.c.md#debug_init>)  (Implementation)


---
### debug\_print\_last\_launched\_app<!-- {{#callable_declaration:debug_print_last_launched_app}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/debug/debug.h#L33>)

Logs the name of the last launched application.
- **Description**: Use this function to log the name of the last application that was launched before a reboot. It is useful for debugging purposes to understand which application was active at the time of a system restart. This function must be called in an environment where logging is enabled, as it relies on the `DEBUG_LOG` macro to output information. It does not require any parameters and does not return any value. Ensure that the system has access to the necessary application information storage to retrieve the last launched app details.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`debug_print_last_launched_app`](<debug.c.md#debug_print_last_launched_app>)  (Implementation)
